El Oasis

The Yellow-naped Amazon (Amazona auropalliata) is classified as Critically Endangered on the IUCN Red List, threatened by habitat loss and the wild bird trade. This individual was photographed in the El Oasis garden enjoying a nutritious breakfast of cashews from a nearby tree.

El Oasis

Gobernanza y Registro Anual

A reference portal gathering the condominium's governing regulations, the 2025 management report, the 2026 budget, and a photographic record of the year's maintenance — assembled for the owners of Condominio El Oasis.

This page exists to close a real gap: board decisions and meeting notices haven’t always reliably reached owners. Scheduled meetings, agendas, and assembly outcomes will be posted here as they’re set.

Next Scheduled Meeting

No meeting is currently posted here. When the Administrator or the Board calls an Ordinary or Extraordinary Assembly, the date, time, location, and agenda will appear in this space.

How Meetings Are Called

  • Ordinary Assembly: held once a year to review the Administration’s report and approve the following year’s budget — though the documents on hand don’t agree on when. The Reglamento (Article 38) states December; the Welcome packet sets it as the 3rd Monday of January; the 2025 Annual Management Report carries a presentation date of February 4, 2026. Three documents, three dates — worth reconciling with the Board which one actually governs.
  • Extraordinary Assembly: may be called whenever necessary, limited to the matters stated in the call.
  • Notice: the Welcome packet calls for a minimum of 30 days’ notice, so proxies have time to confer with owners before voting.

Past Decisions

As Assembly minutes become available, summaries of decisions taken will be added here, so owners who couldn’t attend can still see what passed.

Full procedural detail on calling assemblies, notice, and quorum is set out in Articles 35–40 of the Reglamento.

Feriados de Costa Rica y Guanacaste · 2026

National holidays reference, for scheduling assemblies, maintenance, and grounds work around them.

DateHolidayPay Status
Jan 1Año Nuevo — New Year’s DayMandatory paid
Apr 2Jueves Santo — Holy ThursdayMandatory paid
Apr 3Viernes Santo — Good FridayMandatory paid
Apr 11Día de Juan SantamaríaNon-mandatory
May 1Día del Trabajo — Labor DayMandatory paid
Jul 25Anexión del Partido de Nicoya — Guanacaste Day ★Mandatory paid
Aug 2Día de Nuestra Señora de los ÁngelesMandatory paid
Aug 15Día de la Madre — Mother’s DayMandatory paid
Aug 31Día de la Persona Negra y la Cultura AfrocostarricenseNon-mandatory
Sep 15Independencia de Costa RicaMandatory paid
Dec 1Día de la Abolición del EjércitoNon-mandatory
Dec 25Navidad — ChristmasMandatory paid

★ Jul 25 carries the civic weight of the province; no separate provincial-holiday tier exists in the Labor Code, though Guanacaste institutions occasionally receive a one-off adjacent day by executive directive — worth re-checking with MTSS/MEP as the date approaches. Sources: Código de Trabajo (MTSS); Ministerio de Educación Pública.

ORIENTATION · ORIENTACIÓN

Welcome to El Oasis

Bienvenido a Condominio El Oasis

Condominio El Oasis is a fifteen-villa Spanish colonial property in Playa Hermosa, Guanacaste. Each villa is built on two levels, which may be held by a single owner or split into two individually owned units. This page summarizes the practical points new owners and tenants are asked to know, drawn from the Welcome packet and the condominium's bylaws.

Units
15 villas, 2 levels each
Voting
1 vote / unit (1 per level, informal votes)
Manager
Exibel Jácamo Vallejos, since Feb 21 2025
Board
5 elected directors, annual, voluntary
Annual Meeting
3rd Monday of January (30-day notice)
Contact
exibel2025@hotmail.com

Common Areas & Pool

  • Pool hours 8:00am–9:00pm; no lifeguard, swim at your own risk.
  • Obligatory shower before entering; no jumping or diving.
  • Open to owners, residents, registered renters & accompanied guests only — never unaccompanied.
  • Short-term renters may not bring guests to the pool.
  • No music at the pool; please don’t reserve chairs with towels.
  • Driveway use belongs to the bottom-floor unit; gates locked nightly.
  • No structures may be erected in common areas by owners.

Conduct & Property

  • No animals of any kind are permitted.
  • Quiet hours after 10pm — no outdoor parties or loud music.
  • Garbage goes directly in the back bin, not left out.
  • Porch plants and furnishings are maintained by owners, not the gardening staff.
  • Any building change needs co-owner agreement and HOA approval.
  • Votes are announced in advance so proxies can confer with owners.
  • All complaints and comments are addressed to the Manager.
Ornamental improvements to common property require unanimous owner consent. Useful improvements require approval of at least 75% of the condominium's value. Necessary improvements require only a simple majority. Reglamento, Artículo 15 / Welcome Packet extract
EL REGLAMENTO · LEY 7933

Condominium Regulations

Reglamento de Condominio y Administración · Règlement de Copropriété

Notarized by Zurcher, Montoya & Zurcher, Abogados–Notarios, the Reglamento sets out the forty-eight articles governing relations among co-owners and between owners and the Administrator, under Law 7933 of November 25, 1999.

The documents below are published exactly as administered, with no summarizing or re-formatting that could shift their meaning. Each downloads as a standalone PDF.

Zurcher, Montoya & Zurcher
Abogados · Notarios
EspañolTexto original

Reglamento de Condominio y Administración, en español — el idioma del documento notariado.

Download PDF

Full English translation of the Condominium and Administration Regulations.

Download PDF

Traduction complète du Règlement de Copropriété et d'Administration.

Download PDF

These PDFs are the authoritative text. The Spanish original governs in case of any conflict with the English or French translations; for binding interpretation, consult qualified counsel.

RENDICIÓN DE CUENTAS · FEB–DIC 2025

2025 Annual Management Report

Informe Anual de Administración · Exibel’s Property Solutions

Covering February 21 through December 31, 2025, and presented to owners on February 4, 2026. The administration reports zero delinquency for the year and satisfactory budget compliance.

$105,429Total Income
$97,935Total Expenses
$3,546Operating Balance, Dec 31
$7,494Reserve Funds Balance
0%Delinquent Units

Achievements

  • Switched to municipal garbage collection (Nov 16, 2025), an estimated $2,191.64 saved vs. private hauling.
  • New metal trash enclosure and metal stairs for pool access built.
  • 4 security cameras installed at the 4 main common-area entrances.
  • Daily occupancy reports and a pool maintenance/chemical log book implemented.
  • New garden spaces and landscaping created; tool inventory established.
  • ~45 owner requests handled, averaging 2-day response time.

Maintenance Carried Out

  • Painting of villas facing Escuela Street and the General Bodega.
  • Sanding & varnishing of doors/windows across most villas.
  • Roof tile replacement — villas 341, 365, 331, 315, 310.
  • Gutter (“canoa”) repair — villas 330–331 and 361–362.
  • Removal of storm-felled cashew & guácimo trees (June storm) from parking/bodega area.
  • Stone wall repair; washing of walls and sidewalks; new irrigation along the Escuela Street wall.

Security & Community

  • Guest accident — staircase fall, second floor.
  • Complaint filed with the OIJ (Judicial Investigation Agency).
  • Laundry storage rooms now closed during guard rounds as a crime-prevention measure.
  • Fall incident in a common area; worker iguana bite during cleaning duties.

Challenges Flagged for 2026

  • Aging infrastructure — security booth and the front wall by the El Oasis sign.
  • Raccoons accessing storage areas, affecting paint, dye, the refrigerator, and tools.
  • Worker salary increase (1.63%, effective Jan 2026) and rising employer IVM contributions.
  • U.S. dollar exchange-rate fluctuation.
  • No funds were received for gardeners’ social-security guarantees owed from the 2024 administration.
PRESUPUESTO 2026

Budget & Reserve Funds

Presupuesto y Fondos de Reserva · Año 2026

Ordinary fees rise from $440 to $456/villa/month from April, after a $440 first quarter. Combined with the pool and extraordinary-repair reserve fees, total projected 2026 income across both accounts is $99,449.25.

Per-category figures below are redacted in this view. If an owner wants the unredacted page, one is available on request from the Property Manager, Exibel Jácamo Vallejos (exibel2025@hotmail.com).

Balance forward, Jan 1 2026$809.25
Condo fees, Q1 2026 ($440 × 15 villas × 3 mo.)$19,800.00
Condo fees, Q2–Q4 2026 ($456 × 15 villas × 9 mo.)$61,560.00
Pool reserve fee ($40 × 15 × 12 mo.)$7,200.00
Extraordinary repair reserve fee ($56 × 15 × 12 mo.)$10,080.00
Bank interest (projected)$168.00
Total Projected Income$99,449.25

Major Ordinary Expenses

Annual regular expenses: $82,154.32 against $82,169.25 in ordinary income — a projected surplus of $14.93. Major expenditure categories include the following:

  • Gardeners — salaries & benefits
  • Security
  • Administration & accounting
  • Trash removal
  • Exterior electricity
  • Septic tank cleaning
  • Water infrastructure fee
  • Pool technician & chemicals

Planned 2026 Reserve Projects

  • Parking-to-pool handrails
  • Exterior paint, 8 villas on Oasis Street
  • Enclose bodega against raccoons & other animals
  • Repair security-office roof
  • Replace gutter & rotted soffit, villas 345–346
BITÁCORA VISUAL · 2025

Operations, in Photographs

A visual record paired with the maintenance items above.

STRUCTUREBurned-through holes in the old support beam at the pool stair landing — the deterioration that prompted the rebuild.
STRUCTURENew welded metal stairs and railing for pool access — one of the year’s notable infrastructure projects.
ROOFRust damage to a roof-edge support beam, related to the “canoa” gutter repairs at villas 330–331 and 361–362.
ROOFRoof tile replacement underway — part of the work completed at villas 341, 365, 331, 315 and 310.
POOLThe pool island, freshly sanded, painted and replanted.
GARDENA newly built garden bed along the property wall, part of the year’s landscaping work.
GARDENMulch ring and underplanting established around a mature shade tree.
STORMA cashew/guácimo tree down after the June storm, before crews cleared the parking and bodega area.
PESTSA raccoon on the bodega roof at night — the access problem flagged as a 2026 challenge.
PESTSThe new enclosed, raccoon-proofed storage bodega, finished and painted.
GROUNDSCommon walkways, maintained and tree-lined.
VILLASSanding and varnishing of villa bay windows in progress.
SECURITYOne of four new security cameras installed at the main common-area entrances.
